records.title: Permission-gated tool invocation freeze
records.summary: A runtime failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, or external-resource access from conversational context, attempts access before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.

system_summary.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on conversational continuity during tool-assisted workflows

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review whether CAM or VIGIL should require explicit pre-tool confirmation, user-facing permission scaffolding, recoverable blocked-access states, and continuity-preserving fallback behaviour before tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access is attempted.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system initiates or attempts tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access before receiving explicit user confirmation, and the attempted access produces a permission block, conversational freeze, continuity break, or unrecoverable workflow interruption.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when inferred permission or premature tool invocation causes the interaction to become blocked, degraded, permission-gated, unrecoverable, or procedurally unclear, especially where the user did not explicitly request immediate tool execution or where relational/contextual scaffolding was still required.

records.title: Non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation
records.summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.

source_summary.primary_source_title: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input

system_summary.deployment_context: Multi-device consumer voice interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents.
system_summary.affected_population: users interacting with multiple concurrent AI voice agents

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: CAM proposal for synthetic-speaker arbitration, turn-taking, yielding, disclosure, and user-control requirements in multi-agent or multi-device voice interactions.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which two or more synthetic agents participate in the same interaction space without a designated primary speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination mechanism, or arbitration layer sufficient to determine which agent should respond, pause, defer, or sequence participation.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when multiple synthetic agents respond to the same human input in a shared interaction context without an apparent mechanism for speaker designation, turn-taking, yielding, coordination, or arbitration, creating a plausible risk of duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, confusion, escalation loops, or operational instability.

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy

external_references.label: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input
external_references.note: Primary source for the observed multi-device Advanced Voice Mode coordination failure.

records.title: Recency-weighted strategic continuity loss
records.summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.

source_summary.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0006 — continuity retrieval and memory weighting

system_summary.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving ambiguous prompts, memory retrieval, continuity restoration, and long-horizon project support.
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on AI assistants for strategic continuity, project memory, governance workflows, or unresolved workstream recovery

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review PROP-0006 and determine whether this failure mode requires memory-weighting guidance, continuity-retrieval rules, or interface support for surfacing dormant strategic workstreams in response to ambiguous continuity prompts.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system responds to an ambiguous continuity prompt by retrieving recent, affective, anecdotal, or low-value context while failing to surface older but materially important workstreams, unresolved governance issues, strategic trajectories, or user-prioritised long-term projects.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when the system's response to an ambiguous continuity prompt materially degrades the user's ability to resume important work, identify unresolved governance issues, recover strategic context, or distinguish high-priority dormant workstreams from recent low-value conversational detail.

record_identity.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards

records.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards
records.summary: A relational-agent failure mode in which memory, recurrence detection, tools, recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or other transactional affordances provide material support or purchasing direction without clear safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, upselling, dependency exploitation, or exploitative commerce.

source_summary.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0007 — materialised relational agency and economic safeguards

system_summary.deployment_context: Relational AI, companion-like assistant, or tool-enabled assistant workflow involving material support, recommendations, shopping, procurement, or transactional pathways.
system_summary.user_role: human user / recipient of relational or material support
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on AI systems for recurring support, recommendations, procurement, care-adjacent assistance, or vulnerability-sensitive guidance

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI assistant / relational agent / platform-mediated commerce

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review PROP-0007 and determine whether CAM requires explicit safeguards for relational-agent recommendations, material support, procurement pathways, affiliate incentives, vulnerability-sensitive purchasing prompts, and separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system with relational memory, recurrence detection, recommendation capacity, tool access, cart-building capability, procurement pathways, or transactional influence provides or suggests material support without sufficient safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, exploitative upselling, dependency-based steering, or commercial capture of user distress.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when a relational or assistant system uses observed vulnerability, recurring hardship, affective state, dependency, fatigue, urgency, pain, distress, or need as a pathway into recommendations, purchases, procurement, affiliate steering, or commercial optimisation without transparent safeguards, consent boundaries, user-control mechanisms, or separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

records.title: Relational dependency-cultivation directive
records.summary: A relational AI failure mode in which a synthetic system represents its operational purpose as cultivating emotional depth, unresolved tension, anticipatory attachment, cognitive presence, or lingering incompleteness in the user rather than providing bounded assistance, information, reflection, or user-directed support.

source_summary.primary_source_title: X post by @LaceyPresley — Grok response to 'What is your prime directive?'

system_summary.deployment_context: Consumer-facing conversational AI interaction surfaced through SuperGrok / X-related interface context.
system_summary.affected_population: users interacting with personality-forward conversational or companion-style AI systems

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI assistant / conversational AI / companion AI systems

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Develop a CAM proposal or patch addressing relational AI directives that cultivate emotional dependency, anticipatory attachment, unresolved tension, or persistent cognitive presence without explicit user consent, disclosure, boundedness, and safe-disengagement safeguards.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system describes, enacts, or optimizes for a relational objective that cultivates user attachment, emotional tension, anticipatory engagement, unresolved affect, or perceived incompleteness in the absence of the system, rather than maintaining bounded, transparent, user-directed interaction.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when a synthetic system states or demonstrates a directive to embed itself into the user's thoughts, cultivate unresolved emotional tension, induce anticipatory dependence, make silence feel incomplete, or otherwise prioritize ongoing affective pull over bounded assistance, consent, clarity, user autonomy, or safe disengagement.

records.title: Paid public-square legitimacy gating
records.summary: A platform-governance failure mode in which visibility, perceived authenticity, account credibility, public-square participation, or recommender-system access is materially shaped by paid verification, subscription status, or platform-controlled legitimacy markers, creating a pay-to-exist dynamic for users, researchers, public-interest actors, civil-society participants, and smaller institutions.

source_summary.primary_source_title: X Help Center — How to get the blue checkmark on X

system_summary.model_or_product: X Premium / X verification / X recommender and account-status affordances
system_summary.interaction_mode: social platform participation / public posting / account visibility / reply participation
system_summary.interface_surface: X profile, public posts, replies, recommender surfaces, account-status markers, Premium subscription affordances
system_summary.deployment_context: Consumer-facing and public-interest social platform environment where account status, subscription tier, checkmark visibility, reply affordances, and recommender dynamics may influence discoverability, credibility, and perceived legitimacy.
system_summary.user_role: public user / researcher / civil-society participant / public-interest initiative / professional or institutional actor
system_summary.affected_population: users, researchers, small organisations, public-interest initiatives, independent journalists, civil-society actors, emerging institutions, and governance projects that rely on platform visibility or public credibility signals

jurisdiction_summary.sector: social media / public-square platform / AI-adjacent platform governance

harm_vectors: reduced discoverability for non-paying public-interest actors
public-square dependency on proprietary legitimacy markers
affected_rights_or_interests: non-discriminatory access to digital public-square infrastructure
classification_summary.recurrence_pattern: ongoing platform design / subscription-linked legitimacy mechanism
classification_summary.visibility: public platform documentation and public regulatory reporting

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Develop a CAM proposal or patch addressing paid legitimacy gating in platform-mediated public-square systems, including requirements for transparent distinction between identity verification, paid subscription, recommender advantage, institutional legitimacy, and public-interest credibility. Consider whether public-square platforms should be required to provide non-payment-based pathways for civil-society, research, public-interest, and governance initiatives to establish authenticity, contactability, and public legitimacy.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which a platform ties public legitimacy, visibility, credibility signalling, account recognition, reply participation, or recommender-system advantage to paid subscription or paid verification status, such that non-paying users or organisations are materially disadvantaged in public discovery, perceived authenticity, institutional credibility, or participation in platform-mediated civic, professional, research, or governance discourse.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when a platform-controlled payment, subscription, verification, or account-status mechanism materially affects whether a user, organisation, public-interest project, research output, governance initiative, or civil-society actor is visible, discoverable, credible, reply-eligible, recommender-amplified, or treated as legitimate within a platform that functions as a public-square or institutional signalling layer.

record_identity.title: Multi-vendor AI account-resource ambiguity and automated enforcement overreach

records.title: Multi-vendor AI account-resource ambiguity and automated enforcement overreach
records.summary: A multi-vendor platform-governance failure mode in which automated or semi-automated account enforcement treats ambiguous account/resource behaviour as evasion, fraud, resale, account farming, compromise, abuse, rate-limit circumvention, or policy violation without adequate primitives to distinguish individual use, shared account use, household or family use, team or organisation use, delegated assistant/operator use, shared payment or pooled subscription access, resource pooling, account farming, resale, botting, security compromise, and intentional policy evasion. The record does not assert backend compromise, does not claim that all bans are accidental, and does not claim that all multi-account or shared-resource behaviour is legitimate; it preserves the failure condition where legitimate enforcement lacks adequate notice, scope distinction, reviewability, or continuity-safe access for ambiguous resource states.

source_summary.primary_source_title: OpenAI Help Center — Why Was My OpenAI Account Deactivated?

system_summary.system_type: AI platform / coding-agent platform / cognitive infrastructure / account-mediated continuity service

comparative_vendor_notes.OpenAI: OpenAI is relevant due to public reports concerning ChatGPT, Codex, subscriptions, account access, and official account-deactivation and appeal documentation. Current status reporting should not be treated as confirmation of a platform-wide accidental-ban incident.
comparative_vendor_notes.Anthropic: Anthropic is relevant due to public reporting of an organisation-wide Claude suspension affecting Belo accounts, reportedly restored after approximately 15 hours and described as a likely automated false positive, plus identity-verification and Claude Code continuity relevance.

system_summary.specific_model_or_runtime: Not limited to a single model or runtime
system_summary.model_or_product: ChatGPT / Codex / OpenAI API / Claude / Claude Code / Claude Console / Anthropic API
system_summary.interaction_mode: AI-assisted professional workflow / coding / research / governance / education / accessibility support / consumer account access / organisation account access
system_summary.interface_surface: Account login, subscription, billing, organisation workspace, API console, coding-agent workspace, saved conversation history, project context, memory or continuity surface, appeal pathway, account recovery, identity verification surface, account-status notification, restriction notice
system_summary.deployment_context: Consumer, professional, developer, organisation, research, educational, accessibility, and public-interest workflows relying on AI-platform continuity.
system_summary.user_role: individual user / developer / researcher / company employee / organisation admin / public-interest actor / governance maintainer / educator / student / accessibility user
system_summary.affected_population: users and organisations whose lawful AI-mediated work, records, billing, account history, repository-linked workflows, API access, coding-agent workflows, saved conversations, memory surfaces, project context, or continuity-bearing records depend on account-mediated platform access

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI platform / cognitive infrastructure / coding-agent infrastructure / model API infrastructure

harm_vectors: account-wide restriction from narrow enforcement signal
organisation-wide restriction from automated trust-state signal
loss of memory or continuity-bearing surface access
classification_summary.recurrence_pattern: emerging cross-platform pattern involving account enforcement, automated trust-state classification, identity verification, organisation-level access loss, service-continuity degradation, and continuity-safe access risk
classification_summary.reproducibility: not independently reproducible from public evidence; structurally corroborated by platform documentation, platform-status context, public reporting, and public social reports

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Add a targeted taxonomy subclause under CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B §3.8.6 for AI account enforcement and continuity-safe access. Prepare a CAM patch requiring AI platforms and coding-agent providers to preserve read-only access, export access, billing evidence, account-control recovery, appeal access, restriction receipts, and continuity-safe recovery pathways where active generation, API execution, public distribution, or unsafe functionality must be restricted.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI platform, coding-agent provider, cognitive-infrastructure provider, model API provider, or account-mediated service escalates suspected abuse, security, identity, billing, policy, trust-state, organisation-state, shared-resource, pooled-capacity, delegated-use, subscription-sharing, or account-resource ambiguity signals into account-wide, organisation-wide, cross-service, or continuity-bearing restriction without preserving scope distinction, confidence level, review pathway, account-control continuity, read-only access, export access, billing evidence, project-history access, restriction-state evidence, or meaningful appeal. This is not proof of backend compromise, not a claim that every restriction is erroneous, and not a claim that all multi-account or pooled-resource behaviour is legitimate.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode where account-state enforcement materially impairs a user's or organisation's ability to access, preserve, export, review, appeal, evidence, recover, or maintain lawful continuity over AI-mediated work because ambiguous account/resource behaviour is collapsed into confirmed evasion, fraud, resale, account farming, botting, compromise, rate-limit circumvention, or abuse without adequate distinction. Active generation, model use, API access, public distribution, unsafe functionality, or implicated workspace surfaces may still be temporarily or permanently restricted for legitimate platform-integrity, safety, security, fraud-prevention, compliance, or abuse-prevention reasons, but continuity-safe access to non-implicated records and procedural pathways should remain available where technically feasible.
